evals.prompt: Build a sign-in form using Keystone UI with email, password, and a submit button. Show validation feedback when the email is invalid.
evals.expected_output: A React component using Form (or <form>) + FieldGroup + Field + FieldLabel + FieldError, with aria-invalid on the input, subpath imports, and semantic tokens.
expectations: Imports Form from '@keystoneui/react/form' OR uses a plain <form> element
Imports Field, FieldGroup, FieldLabel, FieldError (or FieldDescription) from '@keystoneui/react/field' — not '@keystoneui/react'
Imports Input from '@keystoneui/react/input' and Button from '@keystoneui/react/button'
Wraps fields in FieldGroup, not a div with space-y-*
Uses Field as the row wrapper with FieldLabel inside
Validation uses aria-invalid on the Input control + FieldError or ErrorMessage for the message — NOT data-invalid on Field
Uses semantic tokens (bg-primary, text-muted-foreground) — never raw colors like text-red-500
Subpath imports only — never imports from '@keystoneui/react' directly

evals.prompt: Create a confirm-delete dialog using Keystone UI. It should have a title, description, Cancel button (outlined), and Delete button (destructive). Use a custom Button as the trigger.
evals.expected_output: A Modal with ModalTrigger using render prop (not asChild), a required ModalTitle for accessibility, and proper compound parts.
expectations: Imports Modal, ModalTrigger, ModalContent, ModalHeader, ModalTitle, ModalDescription, ModalFooter, ModalClose from '@keystoneui/react/modal'
ModalTrigger uses the render prop: render={<Button />} — NEVER asChild={true}
Includes ModalTitle (visible or with className='sr-only') for accessibility
Cancel button uses variant='secondary' or variant='outline'
Delete button uses variant='destructive'
Uses lucide-react for any icons (no @iconify, no @remixicon in app code)
No sizing classes on icons inside Button (no size-4, w-4, h-4)
No manual z-index on ModalContent — overlay components manage their own stacking

evals.prompt: Build a dashboard with 4 stat cards in a grid. Each card has a title, a large number, a status badge showing a percentage change, and a skeleton loading state. Use Keystone UI.
evals.expected_output: Cards using full Card composition (CardHeader/CardTitle/CardDescription/CardContent), Badge for status, Skeleton for loading, gap-* spacing, semantic colors.
expectations: Imports Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent from '@keystoneui/react/card' — uses full composition, not dumping content into CardContent
Uses Badge from '@keystoneui/react/badge' for the percentage change — not a custom styled span
Uses Skeleton from '@keystoneui/react/skeleton' for loading placeholders — not a custom animate-pulse div
Uses gap-* (gap-4, gap-6) for spacing — never space-y-* / space-x-*
Uses size-* when width and height are equal (size-10) — not w-10 h-10
Uses semantic tokens (text-muted-foreground, text-destructive) — never raw colors like text-red-600 or text-emerald-500
No manual dark: color overrides — semantic tokens already adapt
